metadata.title:
Ne HUNDRED AND FIFTY THOUSAND DOLLAR FIRE


is THE RESULT OF FOSSIL IN CHARGE OF WATER DEPARTMENT

pages.raw_text:
Once more the property owners and
business men of Spokane were COM
PEELED to stand idly by and witness
the spectacle Of $4,000 worth Of
stock and property go up in smoke
while the hard working, exhausted
fire department were making her.
culean efforts to secure a supply of
water sufficient to prevent wide
spread confiagration. That the de-
partment, with the use Of three fire
steamers, finally succeeded in get
ting pressure sufficient to prevent the
spread of fierce flames through the
downtown business district Is due to
the fremen and their leaders and not
to the handling of the water works
system.


At 4:4O o'clock this morning an
alarm Of fire was turned in from
the passenger depot Of the Occur
d'Alene Traction company. at Sprague
avenue and Washington street. over
the telephone, to the effect that the
smokehouse of the Cold Storage mar
ket. 315 to 81 Riverside avenue, was
burning.


The alarm was turned in by the
engineer running the ice machine at
the cold storage plant, who left his
post and ran to the depot to get a
telephone connection, although there
was telephone in the market.


Assistant Fire Chief Joyce respond
ed with No. and No companies
and found the smokehouse, to the
rear of the building. near Sprague
avenue. a mass sof flames.


Mo Water.


Three lines of hose were laid at
once by the firemen. but the water
pressure was SO low that it was found
impossible to make any Roadway
against the rapidly spreading flames.


Assistant Chief Joyce grasped the
situation and at once turned in gen.
eral and special alarm, to which all
of the steamers and fire companies
and Chief Myers responded.


More lines were laid from the n.y.
drants, but these also were almost
useless owing to no pressure. Then
the big steamer from central sta
tion and the two smaller steamers
arrived.


The water was shut of until three
leads could be connected with the big
er steamers. From that time on,
